headlines.title: Nikkei 225 Tops 65,000 First Time on Iran Peace Hopes
Global shares gain and oil prices fall after Trump says talks on ending the war are proceeding
Japanese Yen: Bearish against US Dollar but momentum slows – UOB
Japan plans 3 trillion yen extra budget amid concerns over fiscal strains
Nikkei 225 stock average tops 65,000 for first time
BREAKING NEWS: Japan gov't to compile over 3 tril. yen extra budget: PM Takaichi
Japan plans 3 trillion yen extra budget amid concerns over fiscal strains

marketTone.ko: 오늘 일본 증시는 니케이 225 지수가 사상 처음으로 65,000선을 돌파하며 강세를 보였습니다. 이는 이란 평화 협상 기대감 등 지정학적 요인으로 인한 글로벌 증시 상승 분위기에 힘입은 것으로 풀이됩니다. 한편, 일본 정부는 3조 엔 규모의 추가 예산 편성을 발표했으나, 이로 인한 재정 건전성 우려가 제기되고 있습니다. 엔화는 미국 달러 대비 약세를 지속했지만, 하락 모멘텀은 둔화되는 양상입니다.
marketTone.en: The Japanese stock market saw a strong performance today, with the Nikkei 225 surpassing 65,000 for the first time, buoyed by global optimism stemming from hopes for peace talks in Iran. This geopolitical catalyst also led to gains in global shares and a fall in oil prices. Meanwhile, the Japanese government announced plans for a 3 trillion yen extra budget, which has raised concerns about potential fiscal strains. The yen remained bearish against the US dollar, though its downward momentum appears to be slowing.
